response: The World's Greatest Super-Heroes anthology showcased one of four superheroes known for speaking the phrase "SHAZAM," whose name was Captain Marvel. Captain Marvel, also known as Shazam, is a fictional superhero appearing in American comic books published by DC Comics. The character was created by artist C. C. Beck and writer Bill Parker in 1939 and first appeared in "Whiz Comics" #2 (cover-dated Feb. 1940). Captain Marvel's alter ego is Billy Batson, a boy who, by speaking the magic word "SHAZAM," can transform into a costumed adult with the powers of superhuman strength, speed, flight, and other abilities.
